blocks.text: The death of such an important member of our community is a great loss. Gary was a trustful and sympathetic fellow, although his life was shabby. His hearing loss broke his spirit. Behind Gary's good-natured eyes was a great inner tragedy. He could no longer engage in music, and music meant the world to him. Larger than life. But now this life is over. Tragically. Mean. Still, the music of your laughter, Gary, will stay with us for as long as at least one of us is alive.

For Leo the war was a work of art. Secret operations, ambushes, complex missions — he was in his element. He grew up in the days of the Great War and knew nothing else. And for a real warrior to die during a fight is true happiness. Leo believed that if he dies with weapons in his hands, the great gods of war will take him to their veteran boarding house, where they will play bingo and polo together. We hope that this is the place our good friend Leo is calling "home".

We did not know so much about the Thing. Pretty much nothing, to be honest. It came from outer space and was very beautiful, black and shiny. It's a pity that we did not discover its purpose. Well, at least we don't need to carry it with us anymore, and it's actually heavy. Oh, just forget it.
